name: supabase

auth_signup.description: Sign up a new user with email/password

auth_get_user.returns: { data: { user } } -- user is null if not authenticated
auth_get_user.important: Always use getUser() on server, not getSession(). getUser() validates the JWT with Supabase.

auth_oauth.description: Sign in with OAuth provider (Google, GitHub, etc.)
auth_oauth.returns: { data: { url }, error } -- redirect user to data.url

auth_callback_route.description: Handle OAuth/magic link callback (Next.js App Router)

select.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('table_name')
  .select('*')
  .eq('column', value)
  .order('created_at', { ascending: false })
  .limit(10);

insert.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('table_name')
  .insert({ column1: value1, column2: value2 })
  .select();

update.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('table_name')
  .update({ column: newValue })
  .eq('id', rowId)
  .select();

rls_policy.code:
-- Enable RLS
alter table public.my_table enable row level security;

-- Users can only read their own rows
create policy "Users read own data"
  on public.my_table for select
  using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- Users can insert their own rows
create policy "Users insert own data"
  on public.my_table for insert
  with check (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- Users can update their own rows
create policy "Users update own data"
  on public.my_table for update
  using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- Users can delete their own rows
create policy "Users delete own data"
  on public.my_table for delete
  using (auth.uid() = user_id);
rls_policy.important: Always enable RLS on tables. Without it, any authenticated user can read all data.

realtime_subscribe.description: Subscribe to real-time changes on a table

storage_upload.description: Upload a file to Supabase Storage

storage_get_url.description: Get a public URL for a stored file

middleware_session_refresh.description: Next.js middleware for refreshing Supabase auth sessions

common_mistakes: Using createClient from @supabase/supabase-js directly in Next.js -- use @supabase/ssr instead
Using getSession() on server components instead of getUser() -- getSession doesn't validate the JWT
Forgetting to enable RLS on tables -- data is publicly accessible without it
Not setting up middleware for session refresh -- sessions expire silently
Using supabase-js v1 patterns (supabase.auth.session()) with v2 SDK
Not handling the auth callback route for OAuth/magic links
Using service_role key on the client side -- only use anon key on client
